title: Rinorea strictiflora
taxonomy: Rinorea strictiflora belongs to the family Violaceae, commonly known as the violet family. This family comprises approximately 800 species of flowering plants, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Rinorea strictiflora, specifically, is a species of flowering plant native to Africa. It is a part of the genus Rinorea, which includes several species of plants with similar characteristics. Within the plant kingdom, Rinorea strictiflora is classified as a dicotyledon, indicating it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage.
